detaileddescription.para: Bitmasked flag indicating which gates are to be computed as instantaneous (i.e., no integration)
conductance without modulation component is different from Gk_ in ChanCommon
Function pointers to specific power calculation function. This is an optimization to avoid the full pow function for simple commonly occurring integer exponents

briefdescription.para: Flag for use of conc for input to Z gate calculations.
Flag to indicate if gates have been created.

detaileddescription.para: Access function for the number of Xgates. Gives 1 if present, otherwise 0. Function for safely creating each gate, identified by strings as X, Y and Z. Will only work on a new channel, not on a copy. The idea is that the gates are always referred to the original 'library' channel, and their contents cannot be touched except by the original.

Utility function for destroying gate. Works only on original HHChannel. Somewhat dangerous, should never be used after a copy has been made as the pointer of the gate will be in use elsewhere. This needs to be virtual as it needs to call subclass specific delete methods for the gate pointer.
Access function used for the X gate. The index is ignored.
Access function used for the Y gate. The index is ignored.
Access function used for the Z gate. The index is ignored.

Assign the local conc_ to the incoming conc from the concentration calculations for the compartment. Typically the message source will be a CaConc object, but there are other options for computing the conc.

memberdef.argsstring: (const Eref &e, double power, double *assignee, const string &gateType)
memberdef.name: setGatePower

detaileddescription.para: Dummy assignment function for the number of gates.

Assigns the Xpower for this gate. If the gate exists and has only this element for input, then change the gate value. If the gate exists and has multiple parents, then make a new gate. If the gate does not exist, make a new gate

Assigns the Xpower for this gate. If the gate exists and has only this element for input, then change the gate value. If the gate exists and has multiple parents, then make a new gate. If the gate does not exist, make a new gate

memberdef.argsstring: (double state, double dt, double A, double B)
memberdef.name: integrate

detaileddescription.para: Returns the state variable for the new timestep based on the internal variables A_ and B_ which were passed in from the gate.

The HHChannelBase is the base class for defining Hodgkin-Huxley type channels, specifically dealing with derivatives used in the HSolver. This is derived from the ChanBase rather than the ChanCommon, since the Zombie classes used in the HSolver will not use the ChanCommon fields.
